Messages.Content:
# Privacy in GxPT
GxPT stores your chats and attachments on your device only (AppData/GxPT/Conversations). Nothing is uploaded until you press `Send`, and requests are sent over HTTPS (encrypted HTTP). However, your messages must be sent to the AI model provider. This is done via OpenRouter, which routes your messages to a model provider. Chat content is governed by OpenRouter and each provider's policies.
# Privacy with OpenRouter
By default, OpenRouter does not store prompts or responses unless you opt in. However, to answer your requests, your messages are sent to a model provider, and each provider has its own privacy and data retention policy. These policies vary widely and can change over time: some providers retain data indefinitely, some retain it for a limited period (and may keep content flagged for policy violations significantly longer), and some operate under a Zero Data Retention (ZDR) policy that stores nothing. Always check each provider's current policy for the specifics.
You can control which providers handle your requests from your OpenRouter dashboard, and OpenRouter offers an account-wide setting that restricts routing to ZDR providers only.
## Models and Providers
Not all models are available from every provider. If you restrict routing to ZDR (or to specific providers), make sure the model you select in GxPT is offered by a provider that meets your requirements - otherwise the request may fail.
## Zero Data Retention in GxPT
GxPT can enforce ZDR for you on a per-request basis, without requiring the OpenRouter dashboard setting:
- **Global default:** Settings has a *Zero Data Retention* checkbox. When enabled, every new conversation starts with ZDR on, and the per-conversation ZDR toggle appears checked and locked.
- **Per-conversation toggle:** A *ZDR* checkbox sits next to the model selector and controls ZDR for the current conversation. When it is on, GxPT asks OpenRouter to route the request only to providers with a Zero Data Retention policy.
- **It locks once used:** The first time you send a message with ZDR on, that conversation is permanently marked as ZDR. Its tab and sidebar entry show a `[zdr]` marker, and messages from that point on show a small `zdr` tag. The toggle then stays checked and disabled, so a conversation can never silently switch from zero-retention back to retained.
- **Earlier messages:** Messages sent before ZDR was turned on are not protected retroactively - only messages at and after the point ZDR engaged are covered, which is why the `zdr` tag appears per message.
OpenRouter treats the per-request ZDR flag as additive with your account and organization settings: it can only ensure ZDR for a request, never turn it off. (OpenRouter does not consider in-memory caching of prompts to be retention under a ZDR policy.)
## Documentation & Privacy Settings
- Privacy & Logging Documentation: https://openrouter.ai/docs/features/privacy-and-logging
- ZDR Documentation: https://openrouter.ai/docs/features/zdr
- OpenRouter Privacy settings: https://openrouter.ai/settings/privacy
